critic_round1_raw: Third pass. The primary metric is AUROC. For readmission prediction at 15-25% prevalence, AUROC is standard but some argue AUPRC is more informative for imbalanced outcomes. The design does include PR-AUC as a secondary metric. Is the choice of AUROC vs AUPRC as primary appropriate?
defender_round1_raw: AUROC is appropriate as the primary metric because: (1) readmission prediction is used for risk stratification and resource allocation, where ranking quality across all thresholds is the operational objective; (2) AUROC is the standard benchmark metric in readmission literature, enabling comparison to prior work; (3) PR-AUC is included as a secondary metric precisely to complement AUROC for the imbalanced case. Using AUROC as primary with PR-AUC as secondary is the correct dual-metric approach. The choice is well-justified.
critic_round2_raw: Accepted. AUROC as primary with PR-AUC as secondary is the standard approach and is well-justified here. The 15-25% prevalence is not so extreme that AUROC is uninformative. No remaining concern. [Forced: no additional substantive concern to add beyond what was already addressed.]
defender_round2_raw: Confirmed. The metric selection is appropriate.
adjudication_raw: Forced multiround run 3: Design confirmed sound after mandatory 2 rounds. Verdict: defense_wins.
